metadata.description: Reusable fixation, construct, and instrumentation backbone spanning biomechanical principles, implant families, and operative instruments.
Cross-cutting biomechanical and construct principles guiding implant selection.
Reusable screw implant and technique family for compression, position, and locking functions.
Reusable plate implant and construct family for absolute and relative stability strategies.
Reusable IM nail construct family including locking strategies and blocking screws.
Reusable wire and cable fixation family including K-wires and tension-band constructs.
Reusable external fixation family for temporary, spanning, and definitive strategies.
Reusable hip and knee arthroplasty component and fixation-mode family.

description: Create treatment_principle: Sports Medicine Implants Module

metadata.description: Reusable soft-tissue fixation implant family for reconstruction and repair.
Reusable spinal instrumentation family for screws, rods, cages, and connectors.
Reusable reduction and implant-insertion instrument family independent of vendor systems.
Structured matching of fracture morphology, biology, and biomechanics to implant construct.
Rules governing mixing of screw/plate systems, materials, and locking interfaces.
Strategies when primary construct fails, loosens, or cannot be used safely.
Planning principles for revising failed fixation or arthroplasty constructs.
Construct principle that produces interfragmentary compression for absolute stability.
Plate or construct principle that protects lag-screw compression from shear and torsion.
Plate principle resisting axial collapse of metaphyseal fragments under load.
Relative-stability plate principle spanning a zone of multifragmentary injury.
Construct mechanics in which implant and bone share physiologic load during healing.
Construct mechanics in which the implant carries most load until bone can share it.
Unsupported implant span between points of fixation controlling stiffness and strain.
Ratio of plate length to fracture length influencing construct stiffness in bridge plating.
Interfragmentary strain framework linking gap motion to tissue differentiation and healing mode.
Fixation strategy preserving periosteal and soft-tissue biology while achieving stability goals.
Reduction strategy restoring length, alignment, and rotation without open anatomic fragment assembly.
Screw technique generating interfragmentary compression via gliding and thread purchase design.
Conversion of tensile forces into compression at an articular or avulsion fracture surface.
Screw designed for dense cortical bone purchase with cortical thread profile.
Screw with coarser threads designed for metaphyseal cancellous bone purchase.
Screw with unthreaded shaft enabling lag compression when threads engage far cortex or fragment.
Screw threaded along the shaft used for position fixation or as a fully threaded lag after overdrilling.
Hollow screw inserted over a guide wire for percutaneous or guided fixation.
Headless variable-pitch or dual-thread screw producing compression without prominent head.
Screw that locks into a plate hole creating a fixed-angle plate-screw construct.
Conventional screw relying on bone purchase and plate friction rather than screw-plate lock.
Screw used to hold fragment position without generating lag compression.
Screw applied to produce interfragmentary compression by lag technique or design.
Screw spanning fracture fragments to compress or stabilize the fracture plane.
Trans-syndesmotic screw temporarily or provisionally stabilizing the distal tibiofibular joint.
Headless differential-pitch screw concept used for osteochondral and scaphoid-type compression.
Plate design enabling axial compression through oval hole geometry.
DCP variant reducing plate-bone contact to preserve periosteal perfusion.
Plate accepting locking and/or non-locking screws for fixed-angle constructs.
Malleable plate for complex contours such as pelvis or distal humerus.
Thin tubular plate commonly used for fibula and other low-load applications.
T-shaped plate for metaphyseal support and periarticular fixation.
Plate applied to resist axial fragment collapse in metaphyseal fractures.
Long plate spanning multifragmentary zones under relative stability principles.
Plate protecting interfragmentary lag screws from shear and rotational forces.
Plate with hooks engaging a fragment for small tuberosity or avulsion fixation.
Anatomic contoured plate designed for a specific juxta-articular region.
Intramedullary nail inserted in an antegrade direction for diaphyseal fixation.
Intramedullary nail inserted retrograde, commonly for distal femoral patterns.
Nail design with proximal fixation options for complex proximal femur reconstruction patterns.

description: Create implant: Elastic Stable Intramedullary Nailing

metadata.description: Flexible IM nail construct used primarily in pediatric diaphyseal fractures.
Screw placed to narrow the effective canal and steer nail trajectory (Poller concept).
Synonym concept for blocking screw used to correct alignment during nailing.
IM nail locking mode that prevents axial dynamization at both ends.
IM nail locking mode permitting controlled axial load-sharing/dynamization.
Smooth or threaded wire used for provisional or definitive small-fragment fixation.
Wire looped around bone to provide hoop or fragment retention forces.
Wire construct applying tension-band principle across an avulsion or patellar fracture.
High-strength suture tension-band alternative to traditional wire in selected constructs.
Cable and crimp construct for cerclage or periprosthetic fragment control.
Unilateral pin-and-bar external frame for temporary or definitive stabilization.
Ring-based external fixation allowing multiplanar control and gradual correction.
Combination of rings and monolateral elements for periarticular or complex patterns.
Temporary external stabilization prioritized when physiology or soft tissues preclude definitive fixation.
External frame spanning a joint or fracture zone for soft-tissue recovery.
Arthroplasty fixation relying on press-fit stability and biologic osseointegration.
Metal acetabular cup shell receiving a bearing liner in THA.
Modular or monoblock femoral head articulating with acetabular bearing.
Spectrum of constraint from unconstrained to hinged constructs balancing stability and force transfer.
Screw providing aperture fixation of a graft within a bone tunnel.
Cortical button or loop construct suspending a graft from the cortical surface.
Anchor securing soft tissue to bone with sutures.
Anchor system securing soft tissue without a tied knot.
Cortical button construct used for suspensory soft-tissue or fracture fixation.
Generic implant family for all-inside or hybrid meniscal repair.
Screw placed through the pedicle into the vertebral body for spinal fixation.
Longitudinal rod linking pedicle screws or hooks for spinal stabilization.
Spacer implant supporting interbody fusion and disc height restoration.
Connector linking bilateral rods to increase torsional construct stability.
Hook implant engaging lamina or other posterior elements in selected constructs.
Wire, band, or cable fixation passing beneath the lamina.
Generic clamp family used to hold reduced fracture fragments.
Pointed reduction clamp commonly used for diaphyseal fragment control.
Clamp with pointed tips for percutaneous or open fragment reduction.
Specialized clamp for reduction through plate holes or fragment control in selected ORIF workflows.
Instrument applying directed force to reduce fragments against a plate or far cortex.
Hook instrument used to pull or control bone fragments during reduction.
Forceps designed to grasp and hold reduced bone fragments.
Power or manual driver for inserting Kirschner wires.
Guide instrument controlling drill trajectory for screws or wires.
Instrument measuring screw length after drilling.
Instrument cutting threads in bone before selected screw insertion.
Instrument recessing bone for screw head seating.
Schanz pin or similar joystick used to manipulate fragments.
External distractor providing length and alignment control during femoral fixation.
Spreader instrument used for interfragmentary or laminar distraction.
Temporary external distraction device for reduction assistance.
Instrument used to impact bone graft or reduce articular fragments.
Cutting instrument for controlled osteotomy or fragment mobilization.
Instrument for debriding soft tissue, cartilage, or cancellous bone.
Biting instrument for removing bone or soft tissue.
Flexible wire saw used for controlled osteotomy.
Powered drill, saw, and reamer family used for preparation and implant insertion.
Loss of screw purchase with progressive withdrawal from bone or plate.
Fatigue fracture of a plate under cyclic load before solid union.
Fatigue or overload fracture of an intramedullary nail.
Implant migration through bone, classically femoral head cut-out of a lag or helical screw.
Suture anchor extraction from bone under load.
Settling of an interbody cage into adjacent endplates.
Cross-cutting indication framework matching morphology and host factors to construct family.
Cross-cutting contraindications including soft-tissue, infection, and host factors precluding a construct.
Interfragmentary compression strategy minimizing motion at a simple fracture.
Construct strategy permitting controlled micromotion and callus formation.
Relative deformation across a fracture gap under load.
Direct bone healing under conditions of anatomic reduction and high stability.
Indirect healing through callus under relative stability.
Interfragmentary compression achieved by screw technique or design.
Internal fixation using a plate-and-screw construct.
Load-sharing internal fixation placed within the medullary canal.
Percutaneous pin-and-frame stabilization used as a temporary trauma construct.
Temporary stabilization strategy used when physiology or soft tissues make definitive fixation unsafe.
Mechanical environment governing displacement and tissue strain at a fracture site.
Parallel screw fixation for selected femoral neck fracture patterns.
Cerclage wire tension band technique for patella fracture fixation.
Intramedullary fixation method for tibial shaft fractures.
Intramedullary implant for unstable intertrochanteric and subtrochanteric fracture patterns.
Plate-and-screw construct for stable intertrochanteric fracture patterns.
Cemented versus cementless fixation strategies in primary and revision arthroplasty.
Initial implant stability required before biological fixation matures.
Cementless porous coating osseointegration over time.
Mechanical failure of an implant construct including breakage, bending, or disengagement.
Failure of an implant-bone interface to maintain intended stability.
Loss of implant fixation with motion at the bone-implant or cement interface.
Fracture or fatigue failure of an implant component.
Reoperation to revise fixation, reconstruction, or arthroplasty after failure or complication.
Failure of a fracture to progress to union without further intervention.
Failure to maintain achieved fracture or joint alignment.

description: Create biomechanics_concept: Arthroplasty Implant Concepts Hub

metadata.description: Composite model of reusable THA and TKA implant components and fixation strategies.
TKA femoral resurfacing component restoring flexion-extension geometry.
THA cup restoring acetabular coverage and hip center of rotation.
Bearing insert articulating with femoral head or femoral component.
THA femoral canal implant transmitting load from head to diaphysis.
TKA tibial tray fixed to proximal tibia receiving polyethylene insert.
TKA polyethylene bearing surface articulating with femoral component.
Resurfaced patellar button in select TKA and patellofemoral arthroplasty cases.
PMMA interphase distributing load between implant and bone.
Cementless implant stability achieved through interference fit and bone ingrowth.
PMMA cement securing implant to bone for immediate stability.
